query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.7 (commit: d47e9f32).

## Issue to Resolve

`SharedFlow.toList(mutableList)`: difference behavior between 1.6.4 vs 1.7.0-Beta
Sometimes, I use this method to write simple test on SharedFlow.
Version 1.6.4 works well.
Since 1.7.0-Beta, this method is deprecated and the implementation seem be wrong

```kotlin
val acc = MutableList()
val job = scope.launch(start = Undispatched) {
  sharedFlow.toList(acc)
}

// later...

job.cancel()
assertContentEquals(
 expected = ...,
 actual = acc
)
```

IMO, the correct impl. is `SharedFlow.toList(acc) = (this as Flow).toList(acc)`

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

+1 to this issue and proposed solution. Seems this commit changed the behavior: https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/commit/f981a1d00a9d75169321f5ef63fed16622c3e44f

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking: Let me understand the issue: `SharedFlow.toList(mutableList)` behavior changed between 1.6.4 and 1.7.0-Beta. The issue is in the `Lint.kt` file where deprecated extension functions were added for Flow operators on SharedFlow. Let me look at the relevant file.

content.text:
I can see the bug clearly. On line 148, `toList()` is called **without passing the `destination` parameter**, so items are collected into a new ArrayList instead of the user-provided `destination` list. Same issue on line 160 for `toSet()`.

Let me verify the signature of the original `Flow.toList()` and `Flow.toSet()`:

conversation_history.name: grep
conversation_history.content:
kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/terminal/Collection.kt:15:public suspend fun <T> Flow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> = toCollection(destination)
[Showing results with pagination = limit: 100, offset: 0]

conversation_history.name: grep
conversation_history.content:
kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/terminal/Collection.kt:20:public suspend fun <T> Flow<T>.toSet(destination: MutableSet<T> = LinkedHashSet()): Set<T> = toCollection(destination)
[Showing results with pagination = limit: 100, offset: 0]

content.text: Now the fix is clear. The `toList` delegation needs to pass `destination`, and same for `toSet`:

content.text:
The fix is clean with no lint errors. 

**Summary of the fix:**

The bug was in `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt`, lines 148 and 160:

1. **`toList`** (line 148): Was calling `(this as Flow<T>).toList()` — ignoring the `destination` parameter. Fixed to `(this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)`.

2. **`toSet`** (line 160): Was calling `(this as Flow<T>).toSet()` — ignoring the `destination` parameter. Fixed to `(this as Flow<T>).toSet(destination)`.

This meant that when users passed a `MutableList` to `SharedFlow.toList(acc)`, the collected items went into a new internal `ArrayList` instead of the user-provided `acc`, so `acc` would always remain empty. The fix simply forwards the `destination` parameter to the underlying `Flow<T>.toList()`/`Flow<T>.toSet()` calls.
